zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert de Cruwys, Lord of Cruwys Morchard於1197年出生在英國英格蘭戴文易亞斯特 安斯時，他的父親Sir Alexander de Cruwys I,Baron Cruwys of Morchard31歲，母親Constance27歲。他至少有1個兒子。他去世於1269年在英國英格蘭戴文可盧斯 莫爾哈德享年72歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Cruwys,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Cruse
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name probably from an unidentified place in Normandy named with Old French crues, crus, creus ‘hollow’. Creuse (in Somme, Picardy) and Cruys-Straëte (formerly in Flanders, now in the French département of Nord), which have the same etymology, are alternative possibilities but seem less likely on geographical and tenurial grounds.
English: perhaps a nickname from Middle English crus(e) ‘bold, fierce’, although the regular development would be to Crowse (rhyming with house).

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Cruwys, Lord of Cruwys Morchard was born in 1197, in East Anstey, Devon, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Alexander de Cruwys I,Baron Cruwys of Morchard, was 31 and his mother, Constance, was 27. He had at least 1 son. He died in 1269, in Cruwys Morchard, Devon, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 72.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert de Cruwys, Lord of Cruwys Morchard est né en 1197, à East Anstey, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Alexander de Cruwys I,Baron Cruwys of Morchard, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Constance, avait 27 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1269, à Cruwys Morchard, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 72 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Robert de Cruwys, Lord of Cruwys Morchard(이)가 1197년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 이스트 앤스테이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Alexander de Cruwys I,Baron Cruwys of Morchard(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Constance(은)는 27세였다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1269년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 크루w이스 모르처드에서 72세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert de Cruwys, Lord of Cruwys Morchard родился в 1197 г. в местности эаст ансти, девон, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Alexander de Cruwys I,Baron Cruwys of Morchard было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Constance, 27. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1269 г. в местности крувис морчард, девон, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 72 лет.
